text:
Wave 1 has **1 feature**, but it requires a worktree — so no parallel agents will be spawned per the instructions:

| Feature | Phase | Action | Needs Worktree |
|---|---|---|---|
| `fix-uat-fail-signaling` | implementation | implement_task | **yes** |

**Wave plan summary:**
- **Milestone:** Run Lifecycle Correctness (`fix-run-lifecycle`)
- **Total features:** 2 (`fix-run-startup-recovery` = released, `fix-uat-fail-signaling` = in-progress)
- **Wave 1** — the only remaining work — needs worktree isolation because it's mid-implementation

Since `fix-uat-fail-signaling` has `needs_worktree: true`, it falls outside the scope of the parallel agent launch pattern (which only covers features that don't require worktree isolation).

To advance it, you can run it with worktree isolation:

```
/sdlc-run fix-uat-fail-signaling
```

Or, if you'd like me to spawn a worktree-isolated agent for it, just say the word and I'll launch it with `isolation: "worktree"`.
